page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176 is a high-capacity server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 176 dedicated vCPUs derived from 88 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 704.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10%. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate matrix operations and cryptography, as reflected in top-tier PassMark extended instructions and encryption test results. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 11,966.36 GB/sec and uncached large block writes at 197.06 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core performance benchmarks, such as Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the average tier.

This server is highly suited for highly parallelized workloads, including large-scale database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference. In LLM inference tests, the instance demonstrates strong prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds remain moderate. Given its high resource density and lack of bundled local storage, the instance offers a specialized hardware profile optimized for compute-heavy, memory-intensive applications that rely on network-attached storage, balancing raw multi-core throughput against single-threaded efficiency.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176 is a high-capacity, compute-focused server featuring 176 dedicated vCPUs across 88 physical Intel Xeon 8481C cores running at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 704.0 GB of RAM but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While single-core performance is average, its multi-threaded capabilities excel in demanding workloads. It is highly suited for high-throughput database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176 is an x86_64 server with 176 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 704 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176 features 176 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 704 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-core-count x86_64 platform delivering top-tier multi-threaded compute performance and high memory bandwidth for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 176 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors at 2.7 GHz
704.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high memory read/write bandwidth
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and matrix math
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for high-throughput database operations and CPU-based LLM inference
